note: Mail Login exposes one config object (mail_login.settings), a decorator of core's user.auth service, a form_alter validator, and one permission. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) since they probe documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site I want users to be able to log in with their email address using the Mail Login module. Which config object and key turn this on, and what is the default?
evals.expected_output: The mail_login_enabled key (default 1, i.e. enabled) on the mail_login.settings config object turns on login by email address.

evals.prompt: With Mail Login, how do I disable username login so users can ONLY sign in with their email address? What happens if someone types a username?
evals.expected_output: Set mail_login_email_only to 1 on mail_login.settings. A non-email identifier is then rejected with an error like 'Login by username has been disabled. Use your email address instead.'

evals.prompt: How does the Mail Login module resolve a submitted email address to a user account during login on Drupal 11?
evals.expected_output: It decorates core's user.auth service (AuthDecorator); lookupAccount() validates the identifier with FILTER_VALIDATE_EMAIL and loads the user by the 'mail' property before authenticating.

evals.prompt: Which Mail Login setting controls whether email addresses are matched case-sensitively during login, and what does turning it off do?
evals.expected_output: mail_login_case_sensitive (default 1) on mail_login.settings. Set to 0 for case-insensitive matching, which is accepted only when exactly one account matches (RFC 5321 permits case-sensitivity).

evals.prompt: I want to change the login form's username field label and description with Mail Login. Which setting must be enabled, and which keys hold the mixed username/email labels?
evals.expected_output: Enable mail_login_override_login_labels; then mail_login_username_title and mail_login_username_description (and the email-only / password-reset variants) set the login form labels. All are translatable.

evals.prompt: Where is the Mail Login configuration form and what permission protects it on a Drupal site?
evals.expected_output: The settings form is at /admin/config/people/mail-login (route mail_login.admin), gated by the 'administer mail login' permission.

evals.prompt: Does the Mail Login module change how users register accounts, or only how they log in? How does it differ from login_emailusername?
evals.expected_output: Mail Login only affects authentication and the login/password-reset forms (by decorating user.auth and altering user_login_form / user_pass); it does not change user registration. Unlike login_emailusername, it adds an explicit email-only mode and case-sensitivity handling rather than registration-side changes.

evals.prompt: How does Mail Login keep Drupal's flood control working when a user logs in with an email address instead of a username?
evals.expected_output: Its validator (mail_login_validate_authentication, added via hook_form_alter before ::validateFinal) translates the submitted email to the canonical username and rewrites the 'name' value so flood control keys on one consistent identifier.
